stmt: There exists an APS with no Godelian fixpoint and with `⊬ ⊠⊤`.
There exists an APS in which `⊤` is not maximal, i.e. `¬ ∀ x, x ≤ ⊤`, and which has a Godelian fixpoint not equivalent to `⊠⊤`.
There exists an APS in which `⊤` is not maximal, a Godelian fixpoint exists, and `⊠⊤` is not equivalent to `⊠⊠⊤`.
There exists an APS in which `⊤` is maximal, i.e. `∀ x, x ≤ ⊤`, but no Godelian fixpoint exists.
The odd-parity generalization of `△` is the family of composites `Δ : Delta` satisfying `Odd (Delta.countReft Δ)`.
The even-parity generalization of `△` is the family of composites `Δ : Delta` satisfying `Even (Delta.countReft Δ)`.

For `Δ : Delta` with `Odd (Delta.countReft Δ)`, classify the APS models in which there exists a `Δ`-fixed point, i.e. an element `x` satisfying `x ≐ Delta.act Δ x`, and determine when such a fixed point is unique.

For `Δ : Delta` with `Even (Delta.countReft Δ)`, classify the APS models in which there exists a `Δ`-fixed point, i.e. an element `x` satisfying `x ≐ Delta.act Δ x`, and determine when such a fixed point is unique.
